Header.Name: Letter-v2
Header.Description: Generic elements for letter-metadata description, compliant with the CIDOC-CRM-based ontology CER (https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#).

Component.name: Letter-v2
Component.Documentation: A single message from a sender to one or more organizations or people.

Element.name: project
Element.Documentation: For internal use to assign a label to a specific project.

Component.name: Identifier
Component.Documentation: Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUrl
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: nameOrTitle
Element.clariah:label: Source
Element.Documentation: The name of a Source. For Sources: Use a user-friendly name to identify the source. Depending on the Source type, this may contain a combination of it's name, year of creation, creator, et cetera.

Element.name: abstract
Element.Documentation: An abstract is a short description that summarizes a CreativeWork.
Element.clariah:label: Summary

Component.name: Sending
Component.Documentation: "This class comprises the sending process of correspondence items involving at least two persons. It has been adapted from the TEI standard for correspondences, specifically drawing from the correspAction element. The aim of this class is to supply information concerning the sending or dispatch of a message."

Component.name: Sender
Component.Documentation: "This property describes the Person (CER17) involved in or responsible for an Editing (Activity) (CER 15) or for a Correspondence Action (CER12), for example sender(s) or receiver(s) of a letter..."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Component.name: ReconstructionActivity
Component.Documentation: The Activity that generated a PersonReconstruction. A Person Reconstruction can either be created by a human researcher or by a software algorithm. In case of a human-made reconstruction, provide information about the time, place and knowledge- input. In case of a software algorithm, provide information about the name, version and configuration settings of the program.

Element.name: typeActivity
Element.Documentation: Register which type of activity was used to make the reconstruction (not from PiCo, t.b.d.)

Element.name: agent
Element.Documentation: The agent (person or organisation) that is responsible for the creation of a person reconstruction.

Element.name: startedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The start time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: endedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The end time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: DateSent
Component.Documentation: "This property associates an instance of CER12 Correspondence Action or CER15 Editing or CER24 Co-Presence with one instance of CER18 Time-Span during which the Correspondence Action (CER12) (like Sending (CER13) or Receiving (CER14)) or the Editing process or the inferred Co-Presence of two historical actors was ongoing. The associated instance of CER18 Time-Span is understood as the real time-span during which the phenomena making up the temporal entity instance were active."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: PlaceSent
Component.Documentation: "This property describes the Place (CER19) where a Correspondence Action (CER12) happened, meaning the sending and receiving place, as well as the where the Co-Presence (CER24) of two histociral actors, inferred through reasoning-processes, occured."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Component.name: ReconstructionActivity
Component.Documentation: The Activity that generated a PersonReconstruction. A Person Reconstruction can either be created by a human researcher or by a software algorithm. In case of a human-made reconstruction, provide information about the time, place and knowledge- input. In case of a software algorithm, provide information about the name, version and configuration settings of the program.

Element.name: typeActivity
Element.Documentation: Register which type of activity was used to make the reconstruction (not from PiCo, t.b.d.)

Element.name: agent
Element.Documentation: The agent (person or organisation) that is responsible for the creation of a person reconstruction.

Element.name: startedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The start time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: endedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The end time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Receiving
Component.Documentation: "This class comprises the receiving process of correspondence items involving at least two persons. It has been adapted from the TEI standard for correspondences, specifically drawing from the correspAction element. The aim of this class is to supply information concerning the receipt of a message."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Component.name: ReconstructionActivity
Component.Documentation: The Activity that generated a PersonReconstruction. A Person Reconstruction can either be created by a human researcher or by a software algorithm. In case of a human-made reconstruction, provide information about the time, place and knowledge- input. In case of a software algorithm, provide information about the name, version and configuration settings of the program.

Element.name: typeActivity
Element.Documentation: Register which type of activity was used to make the reconstruction (not from PiCo, t.b.d.)

Element.name: agent
Element.Documentation: The agent (person or organisation) that is responsible for the creation of a person reconstruction.

Element.name: startedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The start time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: endedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The end time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: PlaceReceived
Component.Documentation: "This class is used to refer to places involved in a Correspondence Action (CER12 Correspondence Action) and places which are mentioned in a letter text (CER4 Index Information). They may have an additional authority record like geo names (CER16 Authority Record) but this is not mandatory. Places are not used to give more information about the provenance of a letter, as this is not within the ontology’s scope."

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). For persons: "This property may only be left blank if a person's name was unknown or if a person was unnamed (e.g. a child that died shortly after being born), in which cases the property pnv:nameSpecification should state "unknown" or "unnamed"."

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Component.name: ReconstructionActivity
Component.Documentation: The Activity that generated a PersonReconstruction. A Person Reconstruction can either be created by a human researcher or by a software algorithm. In case of a human-made reconstruction, provide information about the time, place and knowledge- input. In case of a software algorithm, provide information about the name, version and configuration settings of the program.

Element.name: typeActivity
Element.Documentation: Register which type of activity was used to make the reconstruction (not from PiCo, t.b.d.)

Element.name: agent
Element.Documentation: The agent (person or organisation) that is responsible for the creation of a person reconstruction.

Element.name: startedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The start time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: endedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The end time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: providerSource
Element.Documentation: the name of the repository or project (e.g., "correspSearch", "Kalliope", "Deutsches Textarchiv")

Element.name: noteType
Element.Documentation: Add any specific types of notes to the controlled vocabulary.
